369	def read_bool_env(name: str, default: bool) -> bool:
370	    """Read a boolean environment variable with a permissive parser."""
371	    raw = (os.environ.get(name) or "").strip()
372	    if not raw:
373	        return default
374	    lowered = raw.lower()
375	    if lowered in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}:
376	        return True
377	    if lowered in {"0", "false", "no", "off"}:
378	        return False
379	    return default
380	
381	
382	def read_non_negative_int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
383	    """Read a non-negative integer environment variable or fall back."""
384	    raw = (os.environ.get(name) or "").strip()
385	    if not raw:
386	        return default
387	    try:
388	        parsed = int(raw)
389	    except ValueError:
390	        return default
391	    return max(0, parsed)

6	import math
7	from typing import Any, Mapping

9	PROBABILITY_SUM_TOLERANCE = 1e-9

11	
12	def _validate_distribution(
13	    distribution: Mapping[str, Any],
14	    *,
15	    name: str,
16	    require_strictly_positive: bool,
17	) -> dict[str, float]:
18	    if not isinstance(distribution, dict):
19	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a dictionary")
20	
21	    if len(distribution) < 2:
22	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must contain at least two outcomes")
23	
24	    normalized: dict[str, float] = {}
25	    for outcome_id, value in distribution.items():
26	        normalized_outcome_id = str(outcome_id)
27	        if not normalized_outcome_id:
28	            raise ValueError(f"{name} outcome ids must be non-empty strings")
29	        if normalized_outcome_id in normalized:
30	            raise ValueError(f"{name} outcome ids must be unique")
31	        if isinstance(value, bool) or not isinstance(value, (int, float)):
32	            raise ValueError(f"{name} must contain numeric values for all outcomes")
33	        probability = float(value)
34	        if not math.isfinite(probability):
35	            raise ValueError(f"{name} must contain finite numeric values for all outcomes")
36	        normalized[normalized_outcome_id] = probability
37	
38	    if require_strictly_positive:
39	        if any(probability <= 0.0 for probability in normalized.values()):
40	            raise ValueError(f"{name} must contain strictly positive probabilities for all outcomes")
41	    else:
42	        if any(probability < 0.0 for probability in normalized.values()):
43	            raise ValueError(f"{name} must contain non-negative probabilities for all outcomes")
44	
45	    if not math.isclose(sum(normalized.values()), 1.0, abs_tol=PROBABILITY_SUM_TOLERANCE):
46	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must sum to 1.0")
47	
48	    return normalized
49	
50	
51	def _validate_target_probability(target_probability: Any) -> float:
52	    if isinstance(target_probability, bool) or not isinstance(target_probability, (int, float)):
53	        raise ValueError("target_probability must be a number")
54	
55	    normalized_probability = float(target_probability)
56	    if not math.isfinite(normalized_probability):
57	        raise ValueError("target_probability must be finite")
58	    if not (0.0 < normalized_probability < 1.0):
59	        raise ValueError("target_probability must be greater than 0 and less than 1")
60	    return normalized_probability
61	
62	
63	def _validate_liquidity(liquidity: Any) -> float:
64	    if isinstance(liquidity, bool) or not isinstance(liquidity, (int, float)):
65	        raise ValueError("liquidity must be a number")
66	
67	    normalized_liquidity = float(liquidity)
68	    if not math.isfinite(normalized_liquidity):
69	        raise ValueError("liquidity must be finite")
70	    if normalized_liquidity <= 0.0:
71	        raise ValueError("liquidity must be greater than 0")
72	    return normalized_liquidity

74	
75	def _validate_quote_inputs(
76	    previous: Mapping[str, Any],
77	    updated: Mapping[str, Any],
78	) -> tuple[dict[str, float], dict[str, float]]:
79	    normalized_previous = _validate_distribution(
80	        previous,
81	        name="previous",
82	        require_strictly_positive=True,
83	    )
84	    normalized_updated = _validate_distribution(
85	        updated,
86	        name="updated",
87	        require_strictly_positive=True,
88	    )
89	    if set(normalized_previous) != set(normalized_updated):
90	        raise ValueError("previous and updated must contain the same outcomes")
91	    return normalized_previous, normalized_updated

94	def rescale_probability_edit(
95	    previous: Mapping[str, Any],
96	    outcome_id: str,
97	    target_probability: float,
98	) -> dict[str, float]:
99	    """Rescale a distribution so one outcome reaches a target probability."""
100	    normalized_previous = _validate_distribution(
101	        previous,
102	        name="previous",
103	        require_strictly_positive=False,
104	    )
105	    normalized_target_probability = _validate_target_probability(target_probability)
106	    normalized_outcome_id = str(outcome_id)
107	
108	    if normalized_outcome_id not in normalized_previous:
109	        raise ValueError("outcome_id must match a known outcome")
110	
111	    other_outcome_ids = [candidate for candidate in normalized_previous if candidate != normalized_outcome_id]
112	    if not other_outcome_ids:
113	        raise ValueError("previous must contain at least two outcomes")
114	
115	    previous_other_total = sum(normalized_previous[candidate] for candidate in other_outcome_ids)
116	    remaining_probability = 1.0 - normalized_target_probability
117	    if previous_other_total <= 0.0:
118	        scaled_others = {
119	            candidate: remaining_probability / len(other_outcome_ids) for candidate in other_outcome_ids
120	        }
121	    else:
122	        scaled_others = {
123	            candidate: normalized_previous[candidate] / previous_other_total * remaining_probability
124	            for candidate in other_outcome_ids
125	        }
126	
127	    updated = {normalized_outcome_id: normalized_target_probability, **scaled_others}
128	    rounding_drift = 1.0 - sum(updated.values())
129	    if rounding_drift != 0.0:
130	        updated[other_outcome_ids[-1]] += rounding_drift
131	    return updated

134	def lmsr_score_delta(
135	    previous: Mapping[str, Any],
136	    updated: Mapping[str, Any],
137	    liquidity: float,
138	) -> dict[str, float]:
139	    """Compute the per-outcome LMSR score delta for a probability edit."""
140	    normalized_previous, normalized_updated = _validate_quote_inputs(previous, updated)
141	    normalized_liquidity = _validate_liquidity(liquidity)
142	    return {
143	        outcome_id: normalized_liquidity * math.log(normalized_updated[outcome_id] / normalized_previous[outcome_id])
144	        for outcome_id in normalized_previous
145	    }

148	def lmsr_expected_edit_cost(
149	    previous: Mapping[str, Any],
150	    updated: Mapping[str, Any],
151	    liquidity: float,
152	) -> float:
153	    """Compute the expected LMSR cost of moving between two distributions."""
154	    normalized_previous, normalized_updated = _validate_quote_inputs(previous, updated)
155	    normalized_liquidity = _validate_liquidity(liquidity)
156	    return sum(
157	        normalized_updated[outcome_id]
158	        * normalized_liquidity
159	        * math.log(normalized_updated[outcome_id] / normalized_previous[outcome_id])
160	        for outcome_id in normalized_previous
161	    )

255	def build_reference_joint_distribution(

257	) -> dict[tuple[str, ...], float]:

282	def brute_force_conditional_marginals(
283	    joint: dict[tuple[str, ...], float],
284	    target_market_id: str,
285	    context: list[dict[str, str]],
286	    market_ids: tuple[str, ...] = REFERENCE_NET_MARKET_IDS,
287	) -> dict[str, float]:
288	    context_assignments = context_assignments_by_market_id(context)
289	    target_index = market_ids.index(target_market_id)
290	    totals = {
291	        outcome["id"]: 0.0
292	        for outcome in server.MARKETS[target_market_id]["outcomes"]
293	    }
294	    context_mass = 0.0
295	
296	    for state, probability in joint.items():
297	        if not state_matches_context(market_ids, state, context_assignments):
298	            continue
299	        context_mass += probability
300	        totals[state[target_index]] += probability
301	
302	    return {outcome_id: probability / context_mass for outcome_id, probability in totals.items()}
303	
304	
305	def brute_force_apply_probability_edit(
306	    joint: dict[tuple[str, ...], float],
307	    target_market_id: str,
308	    outcome_id: str,
309	    probability: float,
310	    context: list[dict[str, str]],
311	    market_ids: tuple[str, ...] = REFERENCE_NET_MARKET_IDS,
312	) -> dict[tuple[str, ...], float]:
313	    context_assignments = context_assignments_by_market_id(context)
314	    target_index = market_ids.index(target_market_id)
315	    before_marginals = brute_force_conditional_marginals(joint, target_market_id, context, market_ids=market_ids)
316	    current_probability = before_marginals[outcome_id]
317	    target_scale = probability / current_probability
318	    non_target_scale = (1.0 - probability) / (1.0 - current_probability)
319	    updated_joint: dict[tuple[str, ...], float] = {}
320	
321	    for state, state_probability in joint.items():
322	        if state_matches_context(market_ids, state, context_assignments):
323	            scale = target_scale if state[target_index] == outcome_id else non_target_scale
324	            updated_joint[state] = state_probability * scale
325	        else:
326	            updated_joint[state] = state_probability
327	
328	    return updated_joint

15	## Non-Interactive Shell Commands
16	
17	**ALWAYS use non-interactive flags** with file operations to avoid hanging on confirmation prompts.
18	
19	Shell commands like `cp`, `mv`, and `rm` may be aliased to include `-i` (interactive) mode on some systems, causing the agent to hang indefinitely waiting for y/n input.
20	
21	**Use these forms instead:**
22	```bash
23	# Force overwrite without prompting
24	cp -f source dest           # NOT: cp source dest
25	mv -f source dest           # NOT: mv source dest
26	rm -f file                  # NOT: rm file
27	
28	# For recursive operations
29	rm -rf directory            # NOT: rm -r directory
30	cp -rf source dest          # NOT: cp -r source dest
31	```
32	
33	**Other commands that may prompt:**
34	- `scp` - use `-o BatchMode=yes` for non-interactive
35	- `ssh` - use `-o BatchMode=yes` to fail instead of prompting
36	- `apt-get` - use `-y` flag

## Comprehensive Bayes Market Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. OVERALL PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Tech Stack:**
- **Backend**: Python 3 (Flask-like, custom HTTP server using `BaseHTTPRequestHandler`)
- **Frontend**: React 18 + TypeScript (Vite build tool)
- **Testing**: 
  - Backend: pytest (Python)

- **Build & Package Management**: npm/pnpm (frontend), no package manager for backend

**Directory Layout:**
```

│   ├── formula_schema.py         # Market formula definitions

├── docs/                         # Documentation

```

---

### 2. MARKET CONFIGURATION

**Config Files & Constants:**
- `/home/kelvin/bayes-market/backend/server.py` (lines 86-138): `INITIAL_MARKETS` dict with pre-loaded markets (m1, m2, m3)
- Market status: "active", "resolved", "closed", "draft" (frozen set, line 140)
- Market sort options: "volume", "liquidity", "created" (line 141)

**Market Data Structure:**

{

  "title": str,
  "description": str,
  "variableId": str,              # Links to Bayesian variable
  "status": "active" | "resolved" | "closed" | "draft",
  "outcomes": [{"id": str, "name": str}, ...],

}
```

**Key Constants:**
- `ACCOUNT_RISK_LIMIT = 100.0` (line 152) - Default account risk capacity
- `max_position_size = 100.0` (line 160) - Max EventTrade position size
- `MAX_EVENT_FORMULA_CLAUSES = 16` (line 189)
- `MAX_EVENT_FORMULA_CLAUSE_LITERALS = 8` (line 190)

### 3. TRADE EXECUTION FLOW

**Entry Points** (/home/kelvin/bayes-market/backend/server.py):
- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit` (lines 5516-5541) → `handle_probability_edit()`
- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/event-trade` (lines 5543-5566) → `handle_event_trade()`

**Probability Edit Flow** (lines 3539-3660):
1. `preview_unconditional_probability_edit()` - Preview impact before execution
2. `min_asset_check()` (lines 3569-3594) - Ensures account won't go negative
3. `create_probability_edit_order()` (lines 3597-3660) - Materializes order
4. `sync_probability_edit_lmsr_state()` (lines 1598-1651) - Updates LMSR ledger
5. `sync_account_risk_state()` (lines 1652-1698) - Updates account risk metrics

**Event Trade Flow** (lines 4618-4684):
1. Normalize payload: `normalize_event_trade_payload()` (lines 3387-3426)
2. Create order: `create_event_trade_order()` (lines 4646-4682)
3. Update exposure: `sync_account_exposure_state()` (lines 1458-1492)

**Position Net Change Calculation** (lines 1411-1433):
```python
def build_event_trade_position_net_change(position, order):
    current_net_size = position.get("netSize") or 0.0
    size = order.get("size")
    side = order.get("side")
    
    if side == "buy":
        signed_delta = size
    elif side == "sell":
        signed_delta = -size
    
    resulting_net_size = current_net_size + signed_delta
    # Returns: currentNetSize, signedDelta, resultingNetSize
```

**Account Risk Endpoints:**
- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/risk` → `get_account_risk()` (lines 1819-1857)
- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` → `get_account_exposure()` (lines 1745-1763)

- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/pnl` → `get_account_pnl()` (lines 2565-2568)

---

### 5. API ENDPOINT STRUCTURE

**Routing Architecture** (lines 5275-5568):
All requests flow through `route_request(method, path, body, headers)` which returns `(payload_dict, status_code)`

**Health & Service Routes:**
- `GET /` → Service index with route listing (line 5286)

- `GET /v1/version` → API version info (line 5301)

**Market API:**
- `GET /v1/markets` - List all markets with filters (status, sort, q)
- `POST /v1/markets` - Create new market
- `GET /v1/markets/{id}` - Get market detail
- `GET /v1/markets/{id}/meta` - OpenGraph preview (line 5363)

- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit` - Probability edit (line 5516)
- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/event-trade` - Event trade (line 5543)
- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/resolve` - Resolve market outcome (line 5435)
- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/close` - Close market (line 5462)
- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/reopen` - Reopen closed market (line 5488)

**Account API:**
- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/risk` - Risk metrics
- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/pnl` - Profit/loss
- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/exposure` - Live positions (EventTrade only)
- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/positions` - Open positions with unrealized P&L

   - Returns `422 "min_asset_violation"` if violation detected
   - **Does NOT apply to conditional edits** (line 3575)

3. **Position Size Limit** (line 160):
   - `max_position_size = 100.0`
   - Applied per EventTrade via `serialize_account_exposure()` (line 1739)
   - Exposed in response as `maxPositionSize` field

4. **Capacity Indicators** (lines 1241-1252):
   ```python
   {
     "limit": float,
     "available": float,           # minAsset
     "consumed": float,            # limit - minAsset
     "utilization": float,         # consumed / limit
     "status": "healthy" | "warning" | "critical"  # Based on utilization
   }
   ```
   - Status determined in `account_capacity_status()` (lines 1229-1239)

5. **LMSR Score Tracking** (lines 1598-1651):
   - Maintains per-market/context LMSR score deltas
   - Score delta = liquidity * log(new_prob / old_prob)
   - Accumulated in `lmsrState.slices[key].scoreByOutcome`
   - Forms basis of risk calculation for probability edits

6. **Rate Limiting** (lines 541-603):
   - Per agent-id rate limiting
   - Window: 60 seconds (line 196)
   - Configurable via `BAYES_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN` env var (line 395)
   - Enforced via `enforce_rate_limit(agent_id)` (lines 541-575)
   - Returns `429 "rate_limit_exceeded"` with `Retry-After` header

7. **Agent ID Authentication** (lines 192-194, 404-543):
   - Header: `X-Bayes-Agent-Id`
   - Enforced for write operations (market_admin, trade_write)
   - Configurable via `BAYES_REQUIRE_AGENT_ID` env var
   - Used for idempotency tracking and rate limiting

---

### 9. KEY FUNCTIONS & IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

**Market Compilation & Inference:**
- `compile_market_for_inference()` (lines 978-1010) - Prepare market for Bayesian inference
- `query_market_marginals_for_inference()` (lines 1020-1037) - Get conditional probabilities
- `query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference()` (lines 1039-1057) - Single outcome probability
- `refresh_market_compile_snapshot()` (lines 1066-1090) - Cache invalidation on market change

**Probability Edit Math:**
- `rescale_probability_edit()` (lmsr.py, lines 94-131) - Proportionally rescale other outcomes
- `lmsr_score_delta()` (lmsr.py, lines 134-145) - Calculate per-outcome LMSR score changes
- `lmsr_expected_edit_cost()` (lmsr.py, lines 148-161) - Total cost of probability transition
- `quote_probability_edit()` (lmsr.py, lines 164-177) - Full quote with costs

**PnL Calculation:**
- `compute_account_pnl()` (lines 2404-2564) - Full account P&L across positions
- `valuation_distribution_for_slice()` (lines 2348-2363) - Distribution-based valuation
- `accumulate_account_pnl_position()` (lines 2389-2402) - Position-level aggregation

**Market Lifecycle:**
- `transition_market_to_resolved()` (lines 4346-4395) - Resolve to outcome
- `transition_market_to_closed()` (lines 4408-4445) - Close without resolution
- `transition_market_to_reopened()` (lines 4471-4502) - Reopen closed market

---

### 10. DATA FLOW & SYNCHRONIZATION

**Write Request Flow:**
```
HTTP Request
  ↓
BayesHandler.do_POST() (line 5723)
  ↓
handle_api("POST") (line 5649)
  ↓
_read_json_body() - Parse JSON (line 5630)
_enforce_write_controls() - Check agent ID + rate limit (line 5622)
  ↓
route_request(method, path, body, headers) (line 5275)
  ↓
handle_probability_edit() OR handle_event_trade() (etc)
  ↓
normalize_*_payload() - Validate input structure
  ↓
Create command: materialize_*_command()
  ↓
Record terminal outcome OR apply order + sync state
  ↓
return {response_dict, status_code}
  ↓
send_json(payload, status) (line 5604)
```

**State Synchronization:**
1. Account Risk (minAsset): Updated in `sync_account_risk_state()` (line 1652)
2. Account Exposure (positions): Updated in `sync_account_exposure_state()` (line 1458)
3. LMSR Ledger: Updated in `sync_probability_edit_lmsr_state()` (line 1598)
4. Market Marginals: Updated directly in `create_probability_edit_order()` (line 3633)
5. Inference Cache: Invalidated via `CacheInvalidationManager` (line 243)

**Idempotency:**
- Key format: `(market_id, account_id, idempotency_key_from_request)` (line 3460)
- Stored in `IDEMPOTENCY_KEYS` dict
- Replayed via `replay_terminal_outcome()` (line 4156) or `replay_comment_post_outcome()` (line 4181)

### 11. CRITICAL CONSTANTS & CONFIGURATION

**Global State Stores** (lines 224-254):
- `MARKETS` - Active market definitions
- `ACCOUNT_RISK` - Account risk metrics (minAsset, LMSR state)
- `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` - Live EventTrade positions
- `ORDERS` - Filled orders history

def materialize_event_trade_command(
    market_id: str,
    normalized_payload: dict[str, Any],
    account_id: str,
    command_id: str,
    submitted_at: str,
    idempotency_key: str | None = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build and persist an event-trade command envelope."""
    command = {
        "schemaVersion": "bayes-command/v1",
        "commandId": command_id,
        "marketId": market_id,
        "accountId": account_id,
        "commandType": "EventTrade",
        "submittedAt": submitted_at,
        "payload": normalized_payload,
        "meta": {
            "source": "api",
        },
    }
    if idempotency_key is not None:
        command["idempotencyKey"] = idempotency_key

    COMMANDS[command_id] = deepcopy(command)
    return command


def create_event_trade_order(command: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Materialize and persist an accepted event-trade order."""
    market_id = str(command["marketId"])
    market = MARKETS.get(market_id)
    if not market:
        raise ApiError(404, "market_not_found", "Market not found", {"market_id": market_id})

    payload = command["payload"]
    literal = payload["formula"][0][0]
    outcome_id = str(literal["outcomeId"])
    price = round_risk_value(query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference(market_id, outcome_id))
    size = round_risk_value(float(payload["size"]))
    timestamp = utc_timestamp()
    order = {
        "id": generate_order_id(),
        "type": str(command["commandType"]),
        "marketId": market_id,
        "accountId": str(command["accountId"]),
        "commandId": str(command["commandId"]),
        "submittedAt": str(command["submittedAt"]),
        "status": "filled",
        "payload": deepcopy(payload),
        "targetMarketId": market_id,
        "targetOutcomeId": outcome_id,
        "side": str(payload["side"]),
        "size": size,
        "price": price,
        "notional": round_risk_value(size * price),
        "createdAt": timestamp,
        "filledAt": timestamp,
    }
    idempotency_key = command.get("idempotencyKey")
    if isinstance(idempotency_key, str):
        order["idempotencyKey"] = idempotency_key
    ORDERS[order["id"]] = deepcopy(order)

def sync_account_exposure_state(order: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Apply one accepted EventTrade order to the live account-exposure projection."""
    account_id = str(order["accountId"])
    market_id = str(order.get("targetMarketId") or order["marketId"])
    outcome_id = str(order.get("targetOutcomeId") or order["payload"]["formula"][0][0]["outcomeId"])
    timestamp = str(order["filledAt"])

    account = ensure_account_exposure_state(account_id, timestamp)
    position = ensure_account_exposure_position(account, market_id, outcome_id, timestamp)
    net_change = build_event_trade_position_net_change(position, order)
    composite_key = account_exposure_position_key(market_id, outcome_id)
    positions = account["positions"]

    if net_change["resultingNetSize"] == 0.0:
        positions.pop(composite_key, None)
        if positions:
            account["updatedAt"] = timestamp
        else:
            ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE.pop(account_id, None)
    else:
        position["netSize"] = net_change["resultingNetSize"]
        position["lastTradePrice"] = round_exposure_value(order.get("price"))
        position["updatedAt"] = timestamp
        position["lastOrderId"] = str(order["id"])
        position["lastCommandId"] = str(order["commandId"])
        account["updatedAt"] = timestamp

    return {
        "accountId": account_id,
        "marketId": market_id,
        "outcomeId": outcome_id,
        **net_change,
    }

def create_market(body: dict[str, Any] | None) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], int]:
    """Create a new market from a POST payload."""
    if not body:
        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "Request body is required")

    title = body.get("title")
    description = body.get("description", "")
    outcomes = body.get("outcomes")
    expires_at = body.get("expires_at")
    liquidity = body.get("liquidity", 10000.0)

    if not title or not isinstance(title, str):
        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "title is required and must be a string")
    if not outcomes or not isinstance(outcomes, list) or len(outcomes) < 2:
        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "outcomes must be a list with at least 2 entries")
    for o in outcomes:
        if not isinstance(o, dict) or "id" not in o or "name" not in o:
            raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "each outcome must have id and name")
    if not expires_at or not isinstance(expires_at, str):
        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "expires_at is required (ISO 8601 string)")

    # Check for duplicate outcome IDs
    outcome_ids = [o["id"] for o in outcomes]
    if len(outcome_ids) != len(set(outcome_ids)):
        raise ApiError(400, "invalid_payload", "outcome IDs must be unique")

    # Generate variable ID from title
    variable_id = title.lower().replace(" ", "_")[:40]
    existing_market = next(
        (market for market in MARKETS.values() if str(market.get("variableId")) == variable_id),
        None,
    )
    if existing_market is not None:
        raise ApiError(
            409,
            "market_already_exists",
            "A market with this title already exists",
            {
                "title": title,
                "variableId": variable_id,
                "existingMarketId": str(existing_market["id"]),
            },
        )

    # Generate market ID
    market_num = len(MARKETS) + 1
    market_id = f"m{market_num}"
    while market_id in MARKETS:
        market_num += 1
        market_id = f"m{market_num}"

    # Uniform prior
    uniform_p = round(1.0 / len(outcomes), 6)
    marginals = {}
    for i, o in enumerate(outcomes):
        if i < len(outcomes) - 1:
            marginals[o["id"]] = uniform_p
        else:
            marginals[o["id"]] = round(1.0 - uniform_p * (len(outcomes) - 1), 6)

    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
    market: dict[str, Any] = {
        "id": market_id,
        "title": title,
        "description": description,
        "variableId": variable_id,
        "status": "active",
        "outcomes": [{"id": o["id"], "name": o["name"]} for o in outcomes],
        "marginals": marginals,
        "liquidity": float(liquidity),
        "volume": 0.0,
        "created_at": now,
        "expires_at": expires_at,
    }

    MARKETS[market_id] = market
    ensure_market_engine_state(market_id)

        "market": deepcopy(market),
